title: Customer Success €30k-€60k salary + commission
description: Salary: € 30,000 to € 60,000 base salary, plus commission or performance bonus Level: Entry level Open to: Graduates, non\-graduates and career changers Join Tangent’s commercial team Tangent is looking for ambitious entry\-level talent to join our commercial team and help more companies hire talented people from socially diverse backgrounds. This is a role working directly for Tangent. You will help us grow by identifying companies that could benefit from hiring through our platform, starting conversations with decision\-makers and building a pipeline of future customers. You do not need previous sales experience or a degree. We are looking for people who are curious, resilient, motivated by our mission and excited to learn. About Tangent Tangent is creating a fairer way for people to apply for jobs. We connect jobseekers with industry expert mentors who volunteer their time to support people with CVs, applications, interview preparation, industry advice and salary negotiation guidance. Our mentors work at leading companies and use their experience to help others navigate the hiring process with more confidence. Too often, access to top jobs depends on who you know, the advice you can afford and the networks you already have. Tangent exists to change that. As our network grows across London, Dublin, Berlin, Amsterdam, New York and beyond, we are building a team that can help more employers discover talented people from a wider range of backgrounds. We live by our product Our mission is so important to us that we have built it into our own hiring process. When you apply through Tangent, we want you to get a feel for what we are building and why it matters. That means giving candidates access to the wider Tangent platform, including our network of over 3,000 volunteer expert mentors. Mentors can support with interview preparation, industry advice, application guidance and salary negotiation advice. This support is optional and is designed to help candidates build confidence, understand the industry and make informed career decisions. Mentor support is separate from our hiring assessment and does not influence whether someone is selected for this role. What you’ll do As a Customer Success Associate, you will help employers succeed on Tangent and make sure they have a great experience using our platform. Your work will include: * Supporting customers\- Act as a day\-to\-day point of contact for employers using Tangent, helping them understand how to get the best results from the platform. * Onboarding new employers \- Guide new customers through the setup process, explain how Tangent works and help them launch successfully. * Building relationships \- Develop strong relationships with hiring teams, recruiters, people teams and decision\-makers. * Helping customers reach their goals\- Understand each customer’s hiring priorities and help them use Tangent to connect with relevant jobseekers. * Solving problems\- Respond to customer questions, troubleshoot issues and work with the team to improve the customer experience. * Driving engagement \- Encourage customers to stay active, post opportunities, review candidates and make the most of Tangent’s network. * Sharing feedback \- Bring customer insights back to the product, sales and marketing teams so we can keep improving Tangent. * Spotting growth opportunities \- Identify where customers could benefit from more support, additional roles or deeper partnership with Tangent. What we’re looking for We are looking for someone who is: * Passionate about Tangent’s mission * Empathetic and good at understanding people’s needs * Confident communicating with customers * Organised and able to manage multiple relationships * Curious and willing to learn * Comfortable solving problems and asking good questions * Interested in startups, customer success, recruitment, technology or social impact * Motivated by helping people and companies succeed You do not need to have worked in customer success before. You do not need a degree. We welcome applications from graduates, non\-graduates and career changers. Training and support This is an entry\-level role, so we will help you build the skills you need to succeed. You will learn how to: * Onboard new customers * Build strong customer relationships * Run effective customer check\-ins * Understand hiring needs * Support customers using a platform * Communicate customer feedback to internal teams * Use customer success tools and CRM systems * Identify opportunities to improve customer outcomes * Communicate Tangent’s mission and value clearly You will also be joining Tangent at an exciting stage of growth, with the opportunity to take on more responsibility as the company scales. What success looks like In your first few months, success will mean learning Tangent’s mission, understanding our customers and building confidence supporting employers on the platform. Over time, you will be expected to: * Help customers launch successfully on Tangent * Build trusted relationships with hiring teams * Increase customer engagement with the platform * Support customers in reaching their hiring goals * Share useful feedback that helps improve Tangent * Identify opportunities to grow customer accounts * Contribute ideas that improve the customer experience Why join Tangent? * Real social impact \- Your work will help more jobseekers access career opportunities with the support of expert mentors. * Career growth \- You will join a growing company with opportunities to progress as the team expands. * Learn sales from the ground up \- This is a chance to build valuable commercial skills in a supportive, mission\-led environment. * Share options \- Be rewarded with share options as Tangent grows. * A mission\-driven team \- Work with a passionate and ambitious team trying to make hiring fairer. Our mission Top jobs too often go to people with existing networks, privilege and access to specialist support. Tangent is helping to change that by connecting talented people from diverse backgrounds with expert mentors and forward\-thinking employers. If you are excited by our mission and want to help more people get hired, we would love to hear from you. Tangent is an equal opportunity employer. We encourage applications from people of all backgrounds, including those who may not have followed a traditional career path.

title: Data Analyst
description: **Strong SQL Skills Required** The Data Analyst will report to the Director Data Intelligence and will use internal and external data to provide actionable insights. This individual will work closely with data scientists, data engineers, software developers and SME’s within the business to ensure the delivery of accurate analytics and reporting. The Data Analyst will derive understanding and insights via detailed analytics that will lead to increased operational efficiency and effectiveness by enabling proactive data\-driven decisions across the organization. **Key Responsibilities:** * Dissect and solve fleet related business problems using analytical and quantitative approaches to identify patterns and correlations in large, complex datasets. * You will provide data backed solutions to questions such as the below: * What is the optimal length of time to hold a vehicle? * How can we reduce vehicle out of service time? * What is the driving factor behind increased vehicle damage rates? * Provide actionable insight to decision\-makers to influence strategy and performance. * Integrate and curate data from a wide range of internal and external sources to develop detailed reports. * Use data visualization tools to explain your results simply and succinctly to technical, non\-technical, and senior audiences. **Required Education** A bachelor's degree or higher in a quantitative discipline such as statistics, mathematics, physics, engineering or computer science. **Required Skills and Experience** * Minimum of 3 years hands\-on experience in a data analyst role. * Minimum of 2 years hands on experience with Databricks (or equivalent). * Strong capabilities in SQL and experience working with structured and unstructured data. * Strong problem solving and critical thinking skills with a proven record for identifying and diagnosing problems, and solving complex problems with simple, logical solutions. * Strong experience with Data Visualization tools such as Tableau * Outstanding verbal, written, presentation, facilitation, and interaction skills, including * \[Nice to Have] Python scripting skills. ***Hertz is an equal opportunity employer, all applicants must have the legal right to work in Ireland (Valid EU Passport / Relevant V* isa).**

title: Data Quality Analyst
description: **About us:** Amach is an industry\-leading technology driven company with headquarters located in Dublin and remote teams in UK and Europe. Our blended teams of local and nearshore talent are optimised to deliver high quality and collaborative solutions. Founded in 2013, Amach was created to solve a specific problem in aviation: too much complexity, too little usable intelligence. We help airlines modernise their operating model using cloud, data and Al\-delivered by teams with deep aviation domain expertise. Our goal is to maximize airlines' operational efficiency by optimizing resource use, reduce costs and increase customer experience and satisfaction. We are seeking a Data Quality Analyst to join a growing Data \& AI Governance team. This is a hands\-on role focused on improving the quality, trust, and accountability of data across the organisation. You will work closely with business stakeholders, data owners, and technology teams to identify data quality issues, perform root cause analysis, support remediation activities, and help drive the adoption of data governance practices. **Location:** Hybrid (2 days per week in Dublin office) **Key responsibilities \& duties include:** * Investigate and resolve data quality issues. * Define and monitor data quality rules and KPIs. * Gather and document business requirements. * Support the adoption of data governance processes and data catalogues. * Collaborate with business and technical stakeholders to improve data quality. * Produce documentation, reporting, and analysis to support governance initiatives. **Required Experience:** * 3\+ years' experience in Data Quality, Data Governance, Data Analysis, or a related field. * Strong SQL and data analysis skills. * Experience working with data quality management and governance programmes. * Strong stakeholder management and communication skills. * Experience creating business and technical documentation. **Desirable:** * Experience with data quality and observability tools such as Monte Carlo, Soda, or Informatica. * Experience with data catalogues such as Atlan, Collibra, or Purview. * Experience with Power BI, Snowflake, Databricks, Jira, and Confluence. **What’s in it for you:** * An opportunity to join a fast\-growing company * Options for career advancement * Learning and development opportunities * Flexible working environment * Competitive salaries based on experience **Equal Opportunity Employer:** Amach is an equal opportunity employer and makes employment decisions on the basis of merit. We celebrate diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees. This job description is intended to convey essential responsibilities and qualifications for this role, but it is not an exhaustive list of tasks that an employee may be required to perform. If you are passionate about driving customer success, advising on strategic solutions, and contributing to product innovation, we would love to hear from you! **Not for you?** Check out all of our open positions in our **careers page** and follow us on **LinkedIn** for future opportunities. P.S. Share this with friends and co\-workers! Don't be afraid they'll steal it from you, if you're amazing and smart we'll find a role for you. We are growing fast and we are always looking for talented people. At Amach, we strive to be an inclusive community of open\-minded individuals with different backgrounds and we are committed to fostering, cultivating and preserving a culture of diversity, equity and inclusion. We strongly believe that a diversity of experience and background is essential to create a fulfilling environment and better solutions for our people and our customers. All Amach employees and contractors are expected to honour this policy and act to ensure that every individual is respected in the workplace. **Your personal data** Amach will process your personal information in accordance with the EU's General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR). We will comply with data protection law and principles, which means that your data will be: * Used lawfully, fairly and in a transparent way * Collected only for valid purposes and not used in any way that is incompatible with those purposes * Relevant to the purposes we have told you about and limited only to those purposes * Accurate and kept up to date * Kept only as long as necessary for the purposes we have told you about * Kept securely If you would like to contact us about your data, please use the following address: info@amach.com

title: Delta V Engineer
description: **Key Responsibilities** * Coordinate the development of automation **software and hardware** to meet New Product Introduction schedules, including **FATs, SATs, commissioning, and validation** . * Develop and implement automation solutions in line with **S88 batch process standards** . * Deliver **robust, compliant, and cost\-effective automation solutions** within agreed timelines for capital projects. * Support the **Commissioning, Qualification, and Validation (CQV) teams** during DeltaV software shakedown and verification. * Design, code, and test **DeltaV software changes** identified during commissioning. * Collaborate closely with other Automation Engineers to ensure milestones are met and **standards/library modules** are consistently applied. **Qualifications \& Requirements** * Minimum **3 years' DeltaV experience** on\-site in a pharmaceutical environment. * Strong experience designing and programming **control systems** with emphasis on DeltaV DCS. * Knowledge of **S88 batch process control** in the (Bio)Pharma sector. * Ability to troubleshoot **hardware configurations, operator interfaces, and databases** . * Familiarity with **GMP standards** ; knowledge of **21 CFR Part 11** is a plus. * Experience in **commissioning and start\-up** of control systems. * Strong **interpersonal and teamwork skills** . * Ability to work effectively in **fast\-paced, project\-driven environments** . * Experience with **PLC, HMI, SCADA, and process instrumentation** is advantageous. **INDFRS2**

title: DevOps Engineer
description: We are partnering with a leading insurance client on a large\-scale transformation programme and are seeking a DevOps / QA Engineer to support the delivery of resilient CI/CD infrastructure, automated testing, and deployment reliability across critical enterprise platforms. This role sits between DevOps, platform engineering, and quality assurance teams, with a strong focus on automation, platform resilience, and continuous improvement. While not a formal management role, this position will suit someone who enjoys taking ownership, driving standards, and acting as a technical lead across CI/CD and QA initiatives. **Key Responsibilities** * Build, optimise, and maintain robust CI/CD infrastructure and deployment pipelines * Develop and maintain automated smoke, integration, performance, and security testing within the deployment lifecycle * Design and implement automated rollback and failover procedures to minimise downtime and improve platform resilience * Partner closely with engineering teams to identify build failures and drive technical remediation activities * Support continuous improvement across deployment automation, testing standards, and release quality * Prepare and deliver weekly system demonstrations covering infrastructure health, performance benchmarks, and security scanning metrics * Contribute to best practices across DevOps, QA automation, and release engineering processes **Required Experience** * 4\+ years’ experience across DevOps, CI/CD engineering, and QA/testing environments * Strong experience with CI/CD pipelines, deployment automation, and version control workflows * Hands\-on experience with automated testing frameworks for integration and infrastructure testing * Strong scripting capability with experience writing reusable automation and verification scripts * Experience troubleshooting deployment failures and supporting remediation activities across engineering teams * Strong communication skills with the ability to work cross\-functionally across technical teams **Desirable Experience** * Experience within enterprise or regulated environments * Exposure to cloud infrastructure and Infrastructure as Code practices * Experience supporting highly available production systems **How to Apply** If you are a DevOps / QA Engineer looking for a role with strong technical ownership please apply with your latest CV.

title: DevOps Engineer (€60k)
description: Some companies use DevOps to ship apps faster. This team is using it to help reshape how future medicines are discovered. We’re working with a fast\-growing deep\-tech company operating at the intersection of AI, computational science, and pharmaceutical innovation. Their platform is tackling one of the biggest challenges in drug development,, using advanced simulation and crystal structure prediction technology to accelerate research that traditionally takes years. Now they’re looking for a DevOps Engineer who loves building systems from the ground up. Not just maintaining infrastructure… but architecting the engine that powers cutting\-edge scientific computing. **This is the kind of role where you’ll:** ⚙️ Build self\-hosted CI/CD systems engineers actually enjoy using 📦 Orchestrate containerised workloads across distributed infrastructure 📊 Design observability stacks that give real\-time visibility into complex systems 🛠 Own infrastructure\-as\-code and automation from day one 🧠 Work directly with highly technical R\&D teams and leadership on scaling architecture 🚀 Help create an environment where experimentation and innovation can move faster, safely They’re not looking for someone who waits for tickets to appear. They want someone curious, hands\-on, and excited by solving hard infrastructure problems in a highly technical environment. **What you’ll likely bring:** • Strong experience in DevOps / Platform Engineering / SRE • Confidence working with Docker, Podman, or container ecosystems • Experience building CI/CD pipelines using GitHub Actions, GitLab CI/CD, Jenkins, or similar • Hands\-on knowledge of observability and monitoring stacks (Prometheus, Grafana, alerting systems, etc.) • Infrastructure\-as\-Code experience with tools like Ansible • Strong Linux fundamentals and scripting skills • Python proficiency for automation and tooling • A self\-starter mindset with the ability to work closely with technical and non\-technical stakeholders alike **Why this role stands out:** This isn’t another “keep the lights on” DevOps role. You’ll be joining a highly innovative, award\-winning environment where infrastructure directly supports scientific breakthroughs, AI\-driven research, and next\-generation pharmaceutical technology. If you enjoy autonomy, technical depth, and building scalable systems that actually matter, this is worth a conversation.

title: Digital Marketing - Acquisition - Dutch Speaker
description: Due to continued expansion and growth, Accenture is currently recruiting for a Partner Manager to drive the rollout of a unique digital marketing advertising solution to EU markets. We are partnering with Pinterest to ensure businesses are set up for success on the platform. You will support new clients from the very start of their Pinterest journey and help to educate, inspire and help advertisers define the best type of campaigns to drive results and achieve their goals. This is an opportunity to work with one of the fastest\-growing players in social media, with the support of a leading global professional services company. Our high\-performing Pinterest team has a proud track record. We maintain a positive, high\-performing team environment by looking out for each other, cherishing diversity and taking time to stop and celebrate success together regularly. **Responsibilities** - We put the power in your hands; to identify, research and contact potential new partner businesses that you believe Pinterest’s unique business proposition could bring value to, delivering personalised product pitches. - Setting the advertiser up for success before passing over to a colleague in Account management to be scaled - You will also work with businesses and brands that have already started advertising on the platform to understand their strategic and tactical goals and co\-create the right marketing plan to achieve them. - Gain an in\-depth understanding of partners' business goals in their respective markets, offering educated insights to help them to succeed. - Developing and growing long term relationships as the ‘face’ \& first point of contact for your clients. - Work toward fixed KPIs \& a quarterly sales target. - Work collaboratively with the wider sales team in order to share (and gain) valuable knowledge on your role. **Basic Qualifications** - Completed High School Diploma (Irish Leaving cert or equivalent) **Qualifications** - At least one year of experience in a target\-driven, customer\-facing role (sales is preferred). - In\-depth, demonstrated knowledge of digital marketing, including understanding of metrics such as CPA, CPC, CPM and ROAS. - Demonstrated experience using analytical approaches to solving business problems. - Demonstrable ability to train and educate advertisers on solutions including how to create and optimise campaigns. - Demonstrable ability to understand, validate, and overcome customer objections by crafting a compelling marketing plan to use Pinterest’s platform \& functionality to achieve advertisers goals. - Ability to learn quickly and maintain current, comprehensive knowledge of the client’s products and services. - Tech\-savvy with an affinity for innovative and emerging technology. - Self\-reliant, adaptable, decisive, and professional; able to effectively multi\-task in a dynamic environment. - Proven experience in working toward quarterly goals, in a KPI\-driven environment. **What do we offer:** - Hybrid role (3 days working from Office and 2 days working from home) - Monday to Friday - Health Insurance - Social team events (indoor and outdoor) - 20 days annual leave \+ 2 Company days \+ 2 volunteers. - Recognition points which you can spend on shopping or vouchers. *Please also note that Accenture is an equal opportunities employer and welcomes applications from all sections of society and does not discriminate on grounds of race, religion or belief, ethnic or national origin, disability, age, citizenship, marital, domestic or civil partnership status, sexual orientation, or gender identity.* *Please advise if you have any particular requirements, so we can look into arranging reasonable adjustments to the recruitment process. You can contact us at* *accessIErecruitment@accenture.com*

title: Digital Media Analyst
description: **Job Description** We’re looking for an ambitious Digital Media Senior Analyst to join our Media \& Marketing Performance team at Accenture Song. This role sits at the intersection of media, data, and consulting \- helping some of the world’s leading brands transform how they plan, activate, and optimise media to drive measurable business outcomes. If you’re interested in digital media, performance marketing, and using data and AI to solve real business problems, this role offers strong exposure, responsibility, and growth. At Accenture Song, you won’t just optimise campaigns, you’ll help shape how media works for the future, combining human insight with AI\-driven decisioning. **What We Do** The Media \& Marketing Performance team helps clients unlock greater value from their media investment through a combination of strategy, data, and technology. We focus on: - Media Consulting: First\-party data strategy, measurement frameworks, and marketing technology - Planning \& Investment: Full\-funnel media strategy, connections planning, and investment optimisation - Performance \& Analytics: Campaign optimisation, experimentation, SEO, and advanced analytics **Your Role** As a **Digital Media Senior Analyst** , you’ll shape and support digital performance strategies for high\-profile clients. Provide strategic guidance across paid search, social, programmatic, and broader digital activity to maximise media investment. You’ll collaborate closely with clients and cross\-functional teams to identify opportunities, solve problems, and deliver measurable growth through digital excellence. **What You’ll Do** **Digital Strategy \& Consulting** - Support the development of integrated, performance\-focused media strategies - Translate data, trends, and insights into clear recommendations - Advise on audience strategy, including the use of first\-party data and segmentation - Contribute to client workshops, strategy documents, and presentations - Stay up to date with trends in media, AI, ad tech, and privacy **Performance Measurement \& Optimisation** - Help design and implement measurement frameworks and KPIs - Analyse campaign performance and identify optimisation opportunities - Support client reporting through dashboards and insights - Contribute to test\-and\-learn agendas and experimentation roadmaps **Client Delivery \& Collaboration** - Work directly with clients and internal teams to deliver projects - Build strong working relationships with stakeholders - Support identification of growth opportunities within accounts - Collaborate across media, data, and creative teams to deliver integrated solutions **Experience \& Skills** - 1 \- 4 years’ experience in digital media, performance marketing, or consulting - Experience across paid media channels - Familiarity with tools such as Google Analytics, Google Ads, Meta Ads Manager, DV360 - Strong analytical skills with the ability to turn data into insights and actions - Understanding of measurement, attribution, and customer journeys - Exposure to first\-party data, ad tech, or martech is a plus **Personal Attributes** - Curious and analytical, with a problem\-solving mindset - Comfortable working in a fast\-paced, evolving environment - Clear communicator, able to present ideas confidently - Collaborative and proactive in team environments - Interested in how data and AI are transforming media and marketing **Preferred Qualifications** - Degree in marketing, business, analytics, or a related field - Certifications in Google Ads, Meta, or analytics platforms - Interest in continuous learning and professional development **What’s in it for you:** At Accenture Ireland, in addition to a competitive base salary, you will also have an extensive benefits package which includes pension, private health insurance, discounted gym membership, emergency caring facilities, and 2 days’ leave per year for charitable work of your choice! Flexible working arrangements can be discussed depending on the position. **Does this appeal to you? If so, we wish to hear from you:** Please advise if you have any requirements, so we can investigate into arranging reasonable adjustments to the recruitment process. You can contact us at accessIErecruitment@accenture.com Accenture is an equal opportunities employer and encourages applications from all sections of society and does not discriminate on grounds of race, religion or belief, ethnic or national origin, disability, age, citizenship, marital, domestic or civil partnership status, sexual orientation, or gender identity, or any other basis as protected by applicable law.

title: Duty Materials Coordinator
description: **Description** Ryanair Holdings plc, Europe’s largest airline group, is the parent company of Ryanair DAC, Lauda, Buzz, Malta Air and Ryanair UK. Carrying over 300m guests p.a. on more than 3,600 daily flights from 95\+ bases, With a fleet of over 610\+ aircraft, with a further 200 Boeing 737’s on order, there is no better time to join Europe’s Largest Airline Group! To meet the challenges and demands that will come with this growth, Ryanair is recruiting for people to join our Materials department. These positions provide an opportunity to develop an exciting career in aviation and to be part of the Ryanair continuing success. The successful candidate will based in our Head Office in Airside Business Park on a 6 on 3 off shift pattern. This is an excellent opportunity for a recent graduate to gain experience in Europes leading airline. **Duties Include** Organising the movement of parts around our network. Providing updates and reporting on the progress of these parts within the suppy chain. Liaising with the different departments within ryanair to support our operation Assisting the materials department with the sourcing of parts. Being a team player and communicating key information relating to important tasks. Assisting in controlling our costs. Assisting in controlling spares requirements for all our aircraft. Monitoring our stock levels through our computer system. Accomplish other such duties as may be reasonably directed by management. **Requirements** * Degree / Experience in a related topic/field * Be an effective communicator * A sound knowledge of computer systems * The ability to work on own initiative * A mature sense of judgement * Be organised and have the ability to multitask * The ability to negotiate **Benefits** * Competitive salary * Discounted and unlimited travel to over 230 destinations * Defined Contribution Pension Scheme * Death in Service Benefit – Up to 2 times of annual basic salary * 20 Days Annual Leave – Increasing to 22 days after 12 months and 25 days after 3 years of continuous service. * Option for up to 5 additional unpaid leave days per year * Cycle 2 Work Scheme * Unrivalled career progression Competencies

title: EMEA Digital Workspace Engineer
description: * we will consider applications from European locations where MetLife has a presence. Warsaw, Poland would be the preference. Our company is currently undergoing a significant transformation across the EMEA region. We operate six regional data centers that play a key role in supporting our test environments. Historically, each region has managed its own processes and infrastructure independently. However, we are now moving toward a more unified, global operating model. The goal of this transformation is to strengthen our internal knowledge base and improve collaboration between regional and global teams. By consolidating expertise and standardizing our practices, we aim to increase efficiency and reduce duplication of effort. This shift will also allow us to provide more consistent and scalable support worldwide. This transformation also focuses on improving collaboration with our internal business partners and responding more effectively to their needs. **The Team You Will Join** The Digital Workspace team, responsible for the desktop experience for users across the region, is part of an infrastructure team focused on transforming and supporting the technology platforms providing critical services to our businesses in around 20 countries across EMEA. **The Opportunity** In this critical role as an EMEA Digital Workspace Engineer, you will be responsible for ensuring the health and performance of all end\-user devices across EMEA, as well as the effective delivery of services and applications to the same. The ideal candidate has strong technical expertise in modern endpoint management tools and a passion for delivering an exceptional technology experience for our colleagues. You will be part of an extended global team and will collaborate with global functions on a daily basis. As an extended team with responsibility for Desktop Experience, Desktop Services, Desktop Application Services and PC Lifecycle you will coordinate and collaborate with these teams to ensure effective title: Equipment Maintenance Technician

As an Equipment Maintenance Technician, you will be responsible for maintaining production and facility equipment to ensure safe, compliant and efficient manufacturing operations. **Key Responsibilities** * Carry out planned preventative and corrective maintenance on production and facility equipment. * Support equipment installation, set\-up, cleaning and machine changeovers between production batches. * Diagnose and troubleshoot equipment faults, escalating issues where appropriate to minimise downtime. * Ensure all maintenance activities are completed in accordance with GMP, SOPs and site quality standards. * Accurately complete maintenance documentation and records in real time. * Support production teams in achieving operational targets and equipment availability. * Safety procedures. * Participate in ongoing training and assist in mentoring new team members where required. * Contribute positively to maintenance projects and departmental improvement initiatives. * Knowledge of cGMP requirements and working within standard operating procedures. * Good computer skills, including Microsoft Word and Excel. * Experience using maintenance management systems such as oclancy@sterlingengineeringeu.com By applying to this job post you agree that Sterling Engineering may process your personal data for recruitment purpose**

title: Event Executive

across the portfolio. * Assist with logistics, suppliers, venues and onsite execution. * Manage guest lists, RSVPs, registration and attendee communications. * Support speakers, sponsors and stakeholder coordination. * Assist with budgets, purchase orders, invoices, and contracts. * Provide onsite event support and post\-event reporting. * Support marketing activity including email campaigns, web updates and social content. **Key Responsibilities \-** **European Motor Show \& Dublin Tech Summit** * Manage ticketing platform, entry logistics and related admin tasks. * Support community partner relationships and collaborative content from outreach to onsite coordination. * Assist with speaker recruitment, programme planning and attendee engagement. * Manage ticketing inbox and partner communications. * Support PR activity including press drops and pre\-event activations. * Coordinate volunteer recruitment, scheduling and communications. * Manage merchandise and promotional stock. * Take ownership of one designated zone onsite. **Essential Skills \& Experience** * 1\+ years’ experience in events, marketing or similar * Demonstrable organisation and multitasking skills. * Excellent communication and attention to detail. * Confident working with clients, suppliers and stakeholders. * Experience with event tools/CRMs is an advantage. * Comfortable title: Experienced Clinical Research Associate - Ireland
description: Join Us on Our Mission to Drive Healthcare Forward IQVIA site management (full service) are seeking experienced Clinical Research Associates in the Republic of Ireland. With access to world\-class training and professional development, we’ll give you the tools you need to create the career you want. Why IQVIA? * IQVIA is recognized as \#1 in its category on the 2026 Fortune® World’s Most Admired Companies™ list for the fifth consecutive year! * We offer genuine career development opportunities for those who want to grow as part of the organization. * The chance to work on cutting edge medicines at the forefront of new medicines development. * IQVIA has access to significant data pools allowing better site selection and recruitment. This helps drive performance of sites making the CRA role more efficient and discussions at site more productive. Next generation clinical development! **Responsibilities** * Perform site monitoring visits (selection, initiation, monitoring and close\-out visits) in accordance with contracted scope of work and regulatory requirements, i.e., Good Clinical Practice (GCP) and International Conference on Harmonization (ICH) guidelines * Work with sites to adapt, drive, and track subject recruitment plan in line with project needs to enhance predictability * Administer protocol and related study training to assigned sites and establish regular lines of communication with sites to manage ongoing project expectations and issues * Evaluate the quality and integrity of study site practices related to the proper conduct of the protocol and adherence to applicable regulations. Escalate quality issues as appropriate * Manage the progress of assigned studies by tracking regulatory submissions and approvals, recruitment and enrollment, case report form (CRF) completion and submission, and data query generation and resolution. May support start\-up phase * Ensure copies/originals (as required) site documents are available for filing in the Trial Master File (TMF) and verify that the Investigator's Site File (ISF) is maintained in accordance with GCP and local regulatory requirements * Create and maintain appropriate documentation regarding site management, monitoring visit findings and action plans by submitting regular visit reports, generating follow\-up letters and other required study documentation * Collaborate and liaise with study team members for project execution support as appropriate **Requirements** * A minimum of 18 months of independent on\-site monitoring * You have successfully managed multiple clinical trial protocols across diverse investigative sites. * In depth knowledge of Good Clinical Practice (GCP) and International Conference on Harmonization (ICH) guidelines * Life science degree educated or equivalent industry experience * Flexibility to travel to sites Apply today and forge a career with greater purpose, make an impact, and never stop learning! * Please note \- this role is not eligible for UK visa sponsorship\* IQVIA is a leading global provider of clinical research services, commercial insights and healthcare intelligence to the life sciences and healthcare industries. We create intelligent connections to accelerate the development and commercialization of innovative medical treatments to help improve patient outcomes and population health worldwide. Learn more at https://jobs.iqvia.com IQVIA is committed to integrity in our hiring process and maintains a zero tolerance policy for candidate fraud. All information and credentials submitted in your application must be truthful and complete. Any false statements, misrepresentations, or material omissions during the recruitment process will result in immediate disqualification of your application, or termination of employment if discovered later, in accordance with applicable law. We appreciate your honesty and professionalism.

title: Fabrication Electrician
description: The Writech Group are an internationally recognised leader in Fire Protection Systems. DESIRABLE CRITERIA * Experience of working with a mechanical and electrical contractor * Train the Trainer or similar Qualification * First Aid \& Fire Warden Certified. installation of various alarm valve skids and pumphouses for client sites. * Testing of various alarm valve skids and modular pumphouses. * Electrical maintenance works carried out throughout Writech HQ where required. * Liaise with onsite Supervisors, Project Engineers, contractors, client Managers, etc. to ensure Client satisfaction is upheld and schedule of works is completed as per timelines. * Cross department collaboration to ensure successful completion of tasks to the standards required. * Develop and implement electrical component checklists and pick lists in conjunction with Project Engineers and Designers. * Provide support and guidance to Project Engineers and Designers on electrical installation policies and procedures. * Update and revise Company Electrical Manuals, SOP’s, Work Instructions in a timely manner when required. * Provide on the job coaching, mentoring and training on electrical processes to colleagues when requested. * Collaboration with Site Supervisors, Design, Engineering and Assembly Mechanical teams to ensure job completion as per client specifications. * Complete any training provided and be open to learning and continual improvement. * Report all accidents, incidents or near misses immediately to your Direct Manager through the App. * Complete daily timesheet in the Writech App. * Submit positive/constructive feedback regularly into the Writech Feedback App. * Wear the required PPE at all the times. * Submit good catches or safety positive feedback regularly into the H\&S Incident Reports section of the Feedback App. * Keeping up to date on all current and future changes of electrical regulations where relevant to the role and implement changes as part of operational excellence. * Other adhoc electrical requests as required by the business. * Promote and maintain a positive HSEQ culture and safe work environment in conjunction with the Writech Health \& Safety Statement. * Proactively engage in all quality initiatives while ensuring best practice standards are promoted and maintained. * Ensure all written documentation is accurate, timely and legible, keeping all records up to date at all times. title: Facilities Assistant
description: **About The Company:** OCS UK \& Ireland is a leading facilities management company with 50,000\+ colleagues and a turnover in excess of £2bn. We deliver innovative, award\-winning services within facilities management, hard services, cleaning, security and catering. Our mission is to make people and places the best they can be for our colleagues, customers and the communities we serve. Our commitment to doing business the right way is rooted in our TRUE values **\- Trust, Respect, Unity, and Empowerment.** **About The Role:** **Days of Working:** Monday to Friday **The Role:** The main purpose of the role is to provide services and duties to maintain the building facilities in consistent ready\-to\-serve conditions by rigid adherence to work generated from requirements and requests, and by vigilance of you core and non\-core responsibilities. To react and generate work orders etc. within stipulated timeframes to ensure maximum Customer Service is achieved for operations. **Responsibilities include:** * Liaising with Facilities Manager and hard services Co\-ordinator to schedule self\-delivery PPMs, Sub\-Contractor PPMs and reactive works. * Assisting FM Team prepare progress reports and provide information for reporting * Coordinating Sub\-Contractor access and work permits issue * Carry out contractor inductions for new contractors coming to site * Respond to calls logged with the facilities helpdesk. Investigate the nature of the problems and seek a speedy resolution. * Work in a flexible manner within the role and to exhibit a fundamental interest in achieving the complete functionality of the building, looking and thinking out of the box to ensure no problem or defect goes undetected. * To undertake repairs, maintenance, service and new works throughout the premises such as fix/hang pictures, notice boards, replace damaged ceiling tiles, replace light bulbs etc. * Assist with the organisation of equipment and furniture for meetings/training/board rooms prior to and after meetings. * Act as contractor service point of contact and coordinate contractor access. * To be first point of call to investigate and deal with facilities issues and emergencies. * Walk the buildings on a daily basis and report any issues noted, resolve issue or if necessary arrange for such items to be resolved. * Ensure all facilities rooms, storage areas and plant rooms are kept in a clean, tidy and functional state. * Perform any other duties that may be reasonably required of you given the broad nature of the role and the time available. * Identify and report on any hazcons **Planned Maintenance Checks:** * Carry out assigned in\-house PPM tasks on site (Daily, Weekly, Monthly, and Annual). * Maintain site records and provide general administrative duties in this regard. * Walk the building daily to ensure all fire exits are clear, extinguishers in place and fire register completed and to check for items/equipment not working. If any issues found, attend to them or report issues. **How to Apply:** If you are interested in joining a business that encourages professional development, career progression and the chance to work with inspirational people then please click apply and register your interest! You can also sign up to our job alerts when registering which will notify you when other suitable vacancies are available. We are an equal opportunities employer and rely on a diverse workforce with a broad range of knowledge, skills, and backgrounds to deliver our goals. We offer an inclusive and welcoming environment and actively encourage applications from all individuals regardless of race, gender, nationality, religion, sexual orientation, disability, or age.

Here at Sysco, our wide, national network aims to serve thousands of cafés, restaurants and bars throughout the country that make up our customers.** Now, we're looking for a Facilities Technician to join our team at our Dublin depot. This role will play a key part in the overall growth of the organisation along with helping to maintain our core ethos here are Sysco \- keeping the customer at the heart of everything we do. This role involves dealing with all contracts and maintenance associated with the Dublin Distribution Centre; supporting environmental initiatives and generally ensuring the safety and wellbeing of our employees will be top priority. To be successful you will have had significant experience in a similar logistics/storage business and understand the issues which come with managing a multi\-temperature facility. Key Accountabilities: * Responsible for delivering a high\-quality maintenance service to operations, must ensure that maintenance works efficiently, effectively and safely * Providing maintenance support, entering data, updating equipment records \& participating in continuous improvement * Electrical \& Mechanical trouble\-shooting * Electro\-mechanical repairs to high\-speed, PLC\-controlled plant, handling and packaging equipment, also pneumatic \& hydraulic control systems * Technical support for the plant facilities and services, including compressed air and refrigeration systems * Performance of preventative maintenance routines * Update the maintenance management system (CMMS), recording all changes, store transactions made \& work carried out * Responsible for completion of your planned work, shifts daily input of all works onto CMMS Requirements: * Possess relevant trade qualification Mechanical/ Electrical/ Automotive Qualification * Minimum 3 years' experience in similar role in regulated manufacturing environment (POST APPRENTICESHIP) * Possess mechanical and electrical maintenance experience and/or qualifications. * Excellent awareness and experience in relation to Quality, Health and Safety * Be familiar with planned preventative maintenance * Experienced in fault finding and problem solving * Flexibility with shift rotation and travel Core Competencies: * Understanding Your Customers \- acts as a role model in personal approach to internal and external customer goals. Successfully delivers messages to all levels of the business and stakeholders achieving the desired effect. On Offer * Competitive salary * Annual Leave * Service Leave * Wellbeing Day * Service Recognition * Learning \& Development * Recognition Awards * Colleague Sysco Discount * Lifestyle Savings Platform on a number of well\-known brands and retailers * Enhanced Company Sick Pay * Employee Assistance Programme, and much more The duties and responsibilities outlined in this job description are neither definitive nor restrictive and may change in detail from time\-to\-time to meet the changing needs of the business.
