title: Technical Specialist
description: **Job Summary** To provide specialist technical support, training, and oversight within a live critical environment, ensuring continuous 24/7/365 operational support across a production data centre facility. The Technical Specialist plays a key role in emergency response, system maintenance \& staff training. Responsibilities include daily planning and coordination of critical activities, ensuring full visibility and coverage across the site. All maintenance\-related documentation and vendor paperwork aligned with the approved annual subcontractor schedule must be prepared and validated in advance to support the operational team. The Technical Specialist is accountable for ensuring these processes are implemented consistently across multiple facilities. **Key Responsibilities** * Maintain continuous uptime of critical systems through hands\-on technical support and proactive supervision. * Lead scheduled and reactive maintenance activities; ensure full compliance via the DFM platform. * Coordinate ongoing maintenance and activities within the Data Centre for facilities. During your day you will have full overview \& awareness of all activities and, in conjunction with operations teams, will prioritize these activities. * Review and approve subcontractor Risk Assessments and Method Statements (RAMS); manage work permits. * Coordinate and oversee day\-to\-day operations and live activities across the facility. * Supervise contractors and vendors, ensuring adherence to site procedures and health \& safety standards. * Deliver technical training to site staff and lead regular scenario\-based "game day" readiness drills. * Assist in the creation and review of SOPs, EOPs, and MOPs; ensure team participation in operational drills. * Maintain accurate records of shift activity, work orders, and system performance via DFM and logs. **Site Interface \& Behaviour Expectations** * Maintain a professional and customer\-focused presence * Work collaboratively with internal teams, vendors, subcontractors, and client reps * Be available to support shift cover during unexpected absence or operational demand. * Comply with all K2 operational, safety, and environmental standards. * Committed to the delivery of excellent customer service. * Calm Manner, able to work under pressure. * Self\-motivated, ability to work on own initiative. * Punctual and reliable. * Good attention to detail. * A team player, able to work with colleagues and clients staff at all levels. **Working environment entails** * Walking job sites which have areas of uneven terrain. * Work at heights. * Regularly lift and/or move items and participate in group lifts. * Work shifts longer than eight hours in duration. * Co\-ordinate body movements when using tools or equipment. * May require bending or twisting the body to access equipment while working. * Involves performing physical tasks all day. * Use hands to manipulate small wires and objects. * Push or pull objects into position. * Work in a noisy environment. * Work in confined spaces. **Key Requirements** * Relevant trade qualification or degree in Mechanical or Electrical Engineering * Strong mechanical background essential – experience with HVAC, AHU, Process water Systems \& mechanical plant operation in critical environments. * F\-Gas registered highly desirable * Proven experience working in live data centres or mission\-critical facilities (highly desirable). * Good working ability on Microsoft Office Products, in particular Excel, Word, Outlook and PowerPoint * Understanding of: + AHUs and ventilation systems + Cooling Systems + Process Water Systems + Chilled water plant and pumps + MV/LV electrical distribution + BMS/SCADA control platforms + Excellent skills in fault diagnosis, report writing, Computer Skills and clear communication under pressure **Education** Trade School Bachelors in Mechanical Engineering or Electrical Engineering

title: Technology Lawyer
description: The market\-leading Technology and Innovation group in Matheson LLP (" **Matheson** ") is seeking to build on its ongoing growth and success. The group, which is the longest established technology practice in Ireland, is acknowledged to be at the cutting edge of technology related legal developments. The group advises on all aspects of technology related legal advice, providing strategic, practical and commercially focused advice to international and domestic clients across multiple sectors. The work includes advising on digital services and content regulation, cybersecurity, artificial intelligence, data protection and privacy matters, intellectual property and strategic and business critical contracts, both transactional and advisory. The group has grown substantially over the last several years and is recruiting ambitious senior associates who are keen to develop their expertise and help manage further continuing growth. **The role** This is a senior role within the Technology and Innovation group, where you will: * Lead on challenging and innovative technology law projects for a variety of clients; * Work collaboratively as part of multi\-disciplinary specialist teams (including Matheson’s Digital Economy Group) on high value and business critical client projects; * Manage junior and associate solicitors on client advisory matters across the full range of technology, digital services and privacy issues; * Participate in a variety of business development opportunities including foreign client relationship visits and representing Matheson at international conferences as well as the possibility of secondment to our London and Californian offices; * Help drive thought leadership in technology law through the Matheson client knowledge programme; and Advise on the legal issues across: * Large scale technology and commercial contract drafting and negotiations; * Data protection, data privacy and data governance; * Artificial intelligence; * Cyber security and operational resilience; * The digital single market including DMA and DSA compliance, content regulation for online platforms and intermediary industries, e\-commerce and online business practices for gatekeepers, platforms and businesses; * Transactional and advisory intellectual property projects. **The person** * You are an experienced Technology Lawyer with a strong track record both technically and in business development. * You are highly client focused and committed to consistently exceeding client expectations. * You have a strong academic track record and broad technical expertise. * You can manage a diverse workload, deliver quality, commercially\-focused advice with minimal supervision, and lead and develop junior lawyers. * You enjoy the law you practice, and are hardworking and resilient. You will thrive in a diverse and inclusive environment where you will work in partnership with top legal talent in a market leading team that is continuing to grow. * You will enjoy establishing relationships with a diverse range of clients and colleagues. * You are enthusiastic about business development, helping develop your junior colleagues to their maximum potential and how you can contribute to managing valuable client relationships as part of the team. **Why join Matheson** At Matheson you have the opportunity to work with exceptional people and a broad range of high calibre domestic and international clients. Working at Matheson provides you with an environment where you can thrive by living our values of Partnership, Respect, Innovation, Diversity and Entrepreneurship. Our Impactful Business Programme is delivered by a number of committees which drive key initiatives around D\&I, CSR, Environmental Sustainability and the Arts. We are also a founding signatory of the Pro Bono Pledge Ireland. We have a hybrid working policy. We provide an excellent working environment, a competitive benefits package and the opportunity to engage in a range of learning and development initiatives which are specifically tailored to your level. We are proud to support the Mindful Business Charter. *Accessibility: If you require any accommodation during the recruitment process, please contact us and we will work with you to ensure the best possible candidate experience.* *We value a diverse and inclusive workforce and are an equal opportunities employer.*

title: Tenure and Promotion
company: Emory University - Office of the Provost
description: * Faculty Affairs * Tenure and Promotion Appointments, Tenure and Promotion Tenure is a covenant between an institution and a faculty member, with responsibilities flowing both ways. The granting of tenure through promotion or appointment is a privilege and an honor both for the University and for the faculty member. It is a declaration to the academic community of Emory’s confidence in the professional abilities and contributions of the faculty candidates, and is among the most critical decisions the University makes. It is also an affirmation by each faculty member of their continued commitment to academic and professional excellence. Promotion marks the development and advancement of scholars as researchers, teachers, and citizens of the academy. It also symbolizes the continued growth and development of the scholar within the academic community. Through appointment or promotion to the rank of full professor, the University recognizes that a faculty member has reached the academic stature meriting highest respect within his or her discipline, as well as full participation in the governance of the University. Emory’s success as a top tier research institution is inextricably linked to the achievements of its exceptional faculty. Their commitment to teaching, research and scholarship is an essential resource for achieving Emory’s vision of an inquiry\-driven, ethically engaged, and diverse intellectual community. University Policies The university policies governing tenure and promotion are outlined in the Board of Trustees' Statement of Principles Governing Faculty Relationships. The Process The tenure and promotion process for tenure\-track faculty involves four sequential steps, outlined below. A nomination for tenure and/or promotion must be approved at each level to advance to the next step in the process. The promotion process for non\-tenure track faculty is governed by each school. The tenure and promotion process originates in Emory's schools and colleges, each of which has its own guidelines and procedures. It is essential for faculty members to become familiar with their school's process and dossier requirements. * Emory College of Arts and Sciences \[PDF] * Oxford College \[PDF] * Goizueta Business School \[PDF] * School of Law \[PDF] * School of Medicine \[PDF] * Nell Hodgson Woodruff School of Nursing \[PDF] * Rollins School of Public Health \[PDF] * Candler School of Theology \[PDF] The Tenure and Promotion Advisory Committee (TPAC) is a panel of faculty members who review candidates' dossiers and make recommendations to the president and provost. * University Guidelines for Candidate Files * Emory OneDrive Login for TPAC Members With advice from TPAC, the president and provost review dossiers for faculty promotions and tenure and make recommendations for promotion and/or tenure to the Academic Affairs Committee of the Board of Trustees. The Academic Affairs Committee of the Board of Trustees reviews candidates' dossiers recommended for consideration by the president and provost. The committee makes its recommendations to the full board (or its Executive Committee when the full board is not in session). The Board of Trustees is the final approving authority for tenure and promotion. Office of the Provost contact: Carol Flowers St. John, Associate Vice Provost for Faculty Affairs/Engagement Tenure and Promotion Advisory Committee (TPAC) The Tenure and Promotion Advisory Committee (TPAC) is responsible for conducting evaluation of all candidates who are nominated for appointment or promotion to associate professor or professor with tenure, or for the grant of tenure at rank, from all departments, if applicable, and from all of Emory's schools and colleges. Download the Guidelines for the Operation of the Tenure and Promotion Advisory Committee TPAC ensures broad representation of academic areas through inclusion of: * 8 school\-based members elected by each school's faculty * 4 at\-large members elected by all tenured faculty, with one each to represent STEM and health sciences, arts and humanities, social and behavioral sciences, and open/unspecified area The committee member term is three years, with one\-third of the panel newly elected each year. The Tenure and Promotion Advisory Committee (TPAC) ensures broad representation of academic areas through inclusion of eight school\-based members elected by each school's tenured faculty and four at\-large members elected by all tenured faculty. TPAC Committee Members Name Representation Term Patricia Brennan At\-Large, Social and Behavioral Sciences 2023\-2026 Rebecca Gary Nell Hodgson Woodruff School of Nursing 2025\-2028 Susan Hylen Cander School of Theology 2024\-2027 Valérie Loichot Emory College of Arts and Sciences 2025\-2026 Ben Lopman Rollins School of Public Health 2023\-2026 Ken Moberg School of Medicine 2023\-2026 James Morey At\-large, Arts and Humanities 2023\-2026 Giacomo Negro At\-large, Unspecified Discipline 2023\-2025 Matthew Sag School of Law 2025\-2028 Jessica Sales At\-large, Unspecified Discipline 2025\-2028 Deric Shannon Oxford College 2025\-2028 Ex Officio Members Name Position Leah Ward Sears Interim President, Emory University Badia Ahad Provost Pearl Dowe Sr. Vice Provost for Academic Affairs * Tenure and Promotion * Promoted and Tenured Faculty * Emeritus Faculty * University Guidelines for Candidate Files

title: Test Analyst
description: We are seeking an experienced IT Test Analyst to join a dynamic technology team responsible for delivering high\-quality digital solutions. The successful candidate will play a key role in ensuring that new systems, enhancements, and integrations meet defined business and technical standards. This is a hands\-on testing role requiring strong analytical ability, a structured approach to quality assurance, and a commitment to excellence. **Role Purpose** The IT Test Analyst will be responsible for planning, executing, and coordinating all phases of software testing across multiple projects. This includes developing test strategies, designing test cases, managing test environments, and collaborating closely with development and business teams to ensure successful delivery. **Core Responsibilities** * Analyse business and technical requirements to understand system functionality and testing needs. * Design and maintain comprehensive test plans, scripts, and scenarios to validate functionality, usability, performance, and security. * Conduct manual and automated testing activities to verify system behaviour and data integrity. * Manage test environments, datasets, and configurations to support accurate and repeatable test execution. * Identify, log, and track software defects through to resolution using appropriate tools and processes. * Provide clear, concise reporting on test outcomes, risks, and quality metrics to stakeholders. * Collaborate closely with developers, analysts, and project managers to resolve issues and enhance solution quality. * Support the continuous improvement of testing processes, tools, and documentation standards. **Experience \& Qualifications** * Minimum 3 years’ experience in a software testing or QA role within a technology\-focused environment. * Relevant third\-level qualification in Information Technology, Computer Science, or a related field. * Proven experience testing web, cloud, or enterprise applications. * Strong knowledge of software testing principles, tools, and methodologies. * Understanding of the software development lifecycle (SDLC) and common delivery approaches such as Agile or Waterfall. * Experience with defect management and test management tools (e.g., JIRA, Azure DevOps, TestRail). * Ability to create and maintain traceability between requirements, test cases, and results. * Excellent analytical, documentation, and communication skills. * Strong attention to detail and a commitment to producing high\-quality work. * Ability to work independently and contribute effectively within a team setting. **Desirable Attributes** * Professional certifications in software testing or quality assurance (e.g., ISTQB Foundation Level or equivalent). * Experience with test automation frameworks or scripting languages. * Exposure to API, integration, or performance testing. * Familiarity with DevOps and CI/CD environments. * Demonstrated initiative, curiosity, and a proactive approach to learning new tools and technologies. **Personal Qualities** * A structured thinker who enjoys problem\-solving and continuous improvement. * Excellent communicator who can translate technical concepts for non\-technical stakeholders. * Reliable, accountable, and focused on delivering results. * Adaptable and comfortable working in a fast\-paced environment with changing priorities.

title: Test Engineer
description: * Demonstrated hands‑on experience with Tricentis Tosca, qTest, and test automation frameworks. * Ability to design, build, and maintain automated test cases for SAP and non‑SAP applications. * Strong understanding of SAP S/4HANA testing cycles, integration points, and regression strategies. * Experience implementing risk‑based and continuous testing approaches using Tricentis tools. * Familiarity with integrating Tricentis into DevOps pipelines and test‑management platforms. * Capable of coaching teams on Tricentis best practices and test‑automation adoption.

title: Theatre Practitioner
description: **Title: Theatre Practitioner** **Location: Dublin** Optical Express, the UK and Ireland’s number one private provider of laser eye, premium intraocular lens and cataract surgery are recruiting for a Theatre Practitioner (RGN) to join our busy surgery team. Operating in our state of the art Dublin clinic, our surgery team is crucial to our patients’ visual outcomes and to the success of Optical Express. If you’re looking for a role that will allow you to continue to learn and progress, read on…. Maybe you are an experienced Theatre Practitioner looking for a new challenge in a different field, or you might be early in your career and want to make your mark in the ophthalmic industry. Providing you have a passion for delivering world class patient care, our environment could be the perfect fit for you. By joining Optical Express, you will be welcomed into an industry leading team which is going from strength to strength. We are continuing to open new state\-of\-the\-art clinics and treatment centres in locations across the UK as well as investing in advanced clinical and diagnostic technologies. For Nurses looking to explore new opportunities, Optical Express has a lot to offer. Our range of ophthalmic treatments mean that our Nurses benefit from real clinical diversity. You will work alongside our expert Ophthalmologists as part of our refractive and cataract surgery multidisciplinary clinical teams, meaning you’ll be involved in leading edge practice, learning from some of the country’s leading specialists in clinical eye care. There will be a requirement to be flexible to work at our other clinics (all travel costs are covered). The role of a Theatre Practitioner is varied, with responsibilities including: * Laser Vision Correction \- Scrub * Intraocular Lens (Cataract) \- Scrub * Anaesthetic assistant * Pre\-operative assessments * Undertake pre\-screening diagnostic duties * Discharge patients with after\-care advice and medications * Medicines management **A career at Optical Express is fulfilling, fun and rewarding. As an employee, you can benefit from the following:** * State of the art, modern working environments * 33 days annual leave\* * Free Laser Eye Surgery and Intraocular Lens Surgery * Free eye tests * Discounted prescription eye wear and sunglasses * Generous Optical Express friends and family discount scheme * Ongoing training and development opportunities * Pension scheme * Access to a range of exclusive offers, discounts and rewards * Employee Assistance Programme and Wellbeing Solutions/Support This is a fantastic opportunity to join a global organisation where you will have the opportunity to grow and develop within a professional working environment. Optical Express is an equal opportunities employer.

title: Thoroughbred Talents Graduate Programme - IT Graduate
description: Our Thoroughbred Talents Graduate Programme (IT Stream) will commence in October 2026\. This is a unique two\-year programme designed to immerse graduates in the dynamic and evolving technology landscape that supports the Irish horse racing industry. Throughout the programme, you will rotate across multiple IT disciplines, gaining exposure to core operational support, software development, project delivery, and cyber security. As part of Horse Racing Ireland (HRI), you will play a key role in supporting and enhancing the digital infrastructure that underpins racecourses, racing operations, and the wider industry. This programme provides a structured pathway to develop both technical expertise and business understanding within a fast\-paced, innovative, and industry\-critical environment. **Programme Rotations** The programme will encompass rotations across the following key areas within the IT department: * IT Shared Services \- Service Desk * Applications Development / Microsoft 365 Technologies * Project Management Office (PMO) \& Continuous Improvement * Cyber Security **IT Shared Services \- Service Desk (1st Line Support)** Graduates will begin their journey in the IT Servicedesk team, providing first\-line technical support across HRI, its racecourses, and supported entities e.g. IHRB. This placement builds essential technical and customer service skills, forming a critical foundation for your IT career. You will: * Provide first\-line technical support to employees across hardware, software, and network issues. * Log, troubleshoot, and resolve service desk tickets within agreed service levels. * Support user onboarding and offboarding processes, including account creation and equipment setup. * Assist with maintaining laptops, desktops, mobile devices, and peripherals. **Applications Development / Microsoft 365 Technologies** In this rotation, graduates will gain exposure to building and enhancing business solutions through either: ***Applications Development:*** * Support the design, development, testing, and deployment of internal systems * Assist in maintaining and improving applications supporting racing and business operations ***Microsoft 365 \& Cloud Technologies:*** * Support administration of Microsoft 365, including SharePoint and Power Platform tools * Assist in automation and reporting solutions using Power BI and Power Automate * Contribute to system upgrades, patching, and maintenance activities * Maintain IT asset and configuration records **Project Management Office (PMO) and Continuous Improvement** Within the PMO, graduates will gain insight into how technology projects are delivered across HRI. You will work as part of a project management team helping deliver a variety of projects and change initiatives the work will. This placement will develop your organisational, analytical, and communication skills. Key duties include: * Assist in the delivery of IT projects and system implementations. * Participate in testing, documentation, and deployment activities. * Identify opportunities to improve processes and user experience. * Contribute to IT documentation, knowledge articles, and training materials. **CyberSecurity** In this rotation, graduates will explore the technologies shaping the future of HRI's IT environment. You will gain hands\-on experience in: * Supporting cybersecurity initiatives and best practices. * Assisting with user access reviews and security audits. * Helping monitor security alerts and escalate potential risks. * Promoting cyber awareness among employees. **Learning \& Development** As part of the programme, you will: * Complete structured training and certification pathways aligned to each rotation * Stay informed on emerging technologies and industry trends * Participate in team workshops, mentoring, and graduate development initiatives * Build both technical capabilities and essential professional skills **Programme Experience** In addition to your rotational placements, you will: * Work across HRI's key locations, mainly in Newbridge (Co. Kildare) and on occasion at selected racecourses such as Fairyhouse, Navan, Leopardstown, and Tipperary * Contribute to IT systems that directly support racing events, operations, and customer experiences * Collaborate with experienced IT professionals and cross\-functional teams * Gain exposure to both operational IT support and strategic technology initiatives * Participate in structured learning and development to build technical and professional skills **Qualifications We Are Seeking:** Essential: * A Level 8 honours degree (or higher) in: * Information Technology * Computer Science * Software Engineering * Cyber Security * Information Systems * Or a related technology discipline (completed within the last two years) Desirable (not essential): * Knowledge of cloud platforms (Azure, AWS, or Google Cloud) * Experience with programming or scripting (e.g. Python, PowerShell, JavaScript) * Understanding of cybersecurity principles * Familiarity with: * ITIL frameworks * Active Directory * Virtualisation technologies * Service desk tools **Key Qualities We Are Looking For** * Passion for Technology: A genuine interest in IT, digital transformation, and emerging technologies. * Interest in Racing: An enthusiasm for working within the horse racing industry and contributing to its continued innovation * Problem\-Solving Skills: Strong analytical ability with a proactive approach to troubleshooting and improvement * Ambitious and Innovative: A desire to learn, grow, and make a meaningful impact * Team Player: Excellent communication skills and a collaborative approach to working with colleagues and stakeholders * Relevant Experience: Internships, academic projects, or extracurricular activities demonstrating technical or analytical capabilities * Adaptability: Ability to work across different systems, teams, and environments in a fast\-paced organisation * Transport: A full driving licence and access to transport is required **Why Join Horse Racing Ireland's IT Graduate Programme?** This programme offers a unique opportunity to develop your IT career within an organisation at the heart of Ireland's horse racing industry. You will gain hands\-on experience across a wide range of IT disciplines while contributing to systems that support one of Ireland's most vibrant and internationally recognised industries. **Selection Process:** * Application Submission: Complete the application questions, providing details about your educational background, relevant experiences, and motivations for joining the graduate programme. * Application Review Our recruitment team will review all applications to assess qualifications, skills, and alignment with the programme's objectives. This stage involves a careful examination of academic achievements, extracurricular activities, and any additional supporting information provided. * Selection Assessments Shortlisted candidates are invited to complete a set of selection assessments designed to evaluate aptitude, analytical skills, and suitability for the specific roles within the graduate programme. * First Round Interviews Successful candidates from the assessment stage are then invited to participate in first\-round interviews. These interviews are typically conducted by the HR team and hiring managers and will focus on exploring your motivations, experiences, and suitability for the programme. * Second\-Round interviews with Senior Management Candidates who successfully navigate the initial interview stage are invited to a second round, where you will have the opportunity to interact with senior management. This stage delves deeper into candidate's potential contributions to the organization and allows senior leaders to assess the alignment of the candidate with the overall strategic goals of the company. * Final Selection After thorough evaluation and consideration of all stages, the final selection is made. Successful candidates will be offered a place on the Thoroughbred Talents Graduate Programme, marking the beginning of your professional journey with the organisation. HRI is an equal opportunities employer, and we are committed to inclusivity and accessibility. We will provide any necessary reasonable accommodation for candidates during the interview process.

title: Treasury Analyst
description: **Treasury Analyst** **Dublin \| Hybrid Working** **Competitive Salary \+ Bonus \+ Benefits** **€50,000 \- €60,000** We are partnering with a leading international organisation to recruit a Treasury Analyst into its Dublin\-based Treasury team. This is an excellent opportunity to join a sophisticated global treasury function supporting operations across multiple countries. The role offers broad exposure to cash management, funding activities, liquidity forecasting, FX, treasury systems, and stakeholder management within a high\-performing international environment. **Key Responsibilities** * Daily cash management and liquidity monitoring across multiple entities * Preparation of cash forecasts and funding analysis * Execution of treasury transactions including intercompany funding and payments * Support FX exposure management and treasury reporting activities * Administration of bank accounts, mandates, and treasury systems * Liaise with finance teams and senior stakeholders across multiple jurisdictions * Support treasury projects, process improvements, and control initiatives **Candidate Profile** * 1\-4\+ years' experience in treasury, banking, financial services, or a related finance environments * Strong understanding of cash management, liquidity, payments, and treasury operations * Experience working with treasury systems, banking platforms, or SWIFT payments and TMS * Excellent analytical, organisational, and stakeholder management skills * Treasury qualifications (ACT) or professional accountancy qualifications are advantageous (ACA, ACCA, CIMA) **What's on Offer** * Competitive salary and annual bonus * Comprehensive benefits package * Hybrid working model * Exposure to a broad range of treasury and funding activities * Opportunity to work within a globally recognised organisation and develop a long\-term career in treasury For a confidential discussion, please get in touch.

title: Warehouse Operative
description: **Warehouse Operative \- Ashbourne** Choice Stores, a rapidly growing retail company, is searching for dedicated and hardworking Warehouse Operatives to join our amazing team. If you're passionate about logistics and looking to take your career to the next level, we want to hear from you! **Responsibilities:** * Accurately pick, pack, and process customer orders in a timely manner * Load and unload delivery vehicles, adhering to safe lifting techniques and guidelines * Maintain stock inventory, ensuring accuracy and organization * Ability to build solid safe pallets * Ensure all deliveries are checked in and out accurately from the warehouse * Ensure that Goods Receiving and Dispatching procedures are followed at all times * Operate warehouse equipment, including forklifts and pallet jacks, in a safe and efficient manner * Participate in routine stock checks and report discrepancies * Work closely with team members to achieve warehouse targets and goals * Comply with all company policies and procedures, including health and safety regulations **Requirements:** * Previous experience in a warehouse or similar role is preferred * Forklift certification is an advantage, but not essential as training will be provided * Excellent organizational and time management skills * Strong attention to detail and accuracy * Ability to work well in a fast\-paced, team\-oriented environment * Physically fit and able to lift and move heavy items safely * Positive attitude and strong work ethic **Why join Choice Stores?** * Competitive salary and benefits package * flexible working hours * Opportunities for growth and career advancement * Supportive and inclusive work environment * Comprehensive training and development programs **Hourly Rate (Age 20\+): €14\.15\-€14\.90 per hour** * Age 19: 90% of the applicable adult rate * Age 18: 80% of the applicable adult rate * Under 18: 70% of the applicable adult rate Don't miss out on this fantastic opportunity to join the Choice Stores family! Apply now and take your career to new heights as a Warehouse Operative.

title: Wealth Management, Business Development Professional, Associate, Dublin
description: **Job Description** Goldman Sachs Private Wealth Management (PWM) specializes in creating comprehensive wealth management plans for ultra high net worth individuals and families, as well as select institutions, including foundations and endowments. PWM Teams work one\-on\-one with clients to advise and deliver customized strategies drawn from our deep investment experience, diverse wealth management capabilities and global reach. Our Private Wealth Advisors (PWAs) deliver an unparalleled investment platform inclusive of the full product and service offerings of Goldman Sachs and beyond, which may include tax and estate planning, philanthropic planning and private banking and lending services. As client needs evolve, this close relationship allows the team to offer complementary services and new opportunities by leveraging the resources of Goldman Sachs and beyond. Our Business Development Professionals (BDP) will provide analytical, investment, and client service support for existing clients while at the same time developing skills to participate in the team’s new business generation under an apprenticeship model with current Private Wealth Advisors. The ongoing training and apprenticeship is designed to prepare our BDPs for a successful transition into our Private Wealth Advisor role. **How You Will Fulfill Your Potential** * Work closely with Private Wealth Advisors to support and implement wealth management strategies for PWM clients * Develop and prepare marketing presentations for prospective clients * Analyze investment portfolios for risk and return characteristics * Execute portfolio rebalancing and trades * Research internal and external investment vehicles for client asset allocation * Provide client service and team support * Source new prospective clients through cold outreach * Serve as team’s client relationship management system expert * Participate in some of the team’s prospective client meetings and follow up, as appropriate * Manage new business team meetings and associated action items * Relay GS Research and ISG market views to Private Wealth Advisors, clients and prospective clients * Liaise with various product groups within Investment Management to respond to client needs **Qualifications** **SKILLS \& EXPERIENCE WE’RE LOOKING FOR** * Bachelor's degree * Related financial services experience of 3\-5 years (preference for prior experience in Wealth Management) * Interest in pursuing a career as a Private Wealth Advisor * Ability to work in a fast\-paced environment and think clearly under pressure * Strong interest in the financial markets and good investment sense/commercial instinct * Ability to multi\-task and work with a variety of partners across the firm * Entrepreneurial spirit coupled with a desire to work in a team\-oriented environment * Excellent verbal and written communication skills * Strong interpersonal skills and ability to build and foster a strong network of relationships * Creative approach to problem solving * Strong project management and organizational skills **About Goldman Sachs** At Goldman Sachs, we commit our people, capital and ideas to help our clients, shareholders and the communities we serve to grow. Founded in 1869, we are a leading global investment banking, securities and investment management firm. Headquartered in New York, we maintain offices around the world. We believe who you are makes you better at what you do. We're committed to fostering and advancing diversity and inclusion in our own workplace and beyond by ensuring every individual within our firm has a number of opportunities to grow professionally and personally, from our training and development opportunities and firmwide networks to benefits, wellness and personal finance offerings and mindfulness programs. Learn more about our culture, benefits, and people at GS.com/careers. We’re committed to finding reasonable accommodations for candidates with special needs or disabilities during our recruiting process. title: Workplace Experience Associate
description: **Company Overview** -------------------- Docusign brings agreements to life. Over 1\.5 million customers and more than a billion people in over 180 countries use Docusign solutions to accelerate the process of doing business and simplify people’s lives. With intelligent agreement management, Docusign unleashes business\-critical data that is trapped inside of documents. Until now, these were disconnected from business systems of record, costing businesses time, money, and opportunity. Using Docusign’s Intelligent Agreement Management platform, companies can create, commit, and manage agreements with solutions created by the \#1 company in e\-signature and contract lifecycle management (CLM). **What You'll Do** ------------------ The WE Associate will be responsible for the front of house \- being the face and personality of Docusign in a friendly, engaging and helpful manner. This position is intentionally called an “associate” and not a “receptionist” because we value the importance of connecting our people with each other, and the outside world. The Associate will thrive in a fast\-paced environment juggling multiple tasks while assisting the WE team with different projects and the smooth and efficient operation of the office. This person will enjoy being flexible in taking on ad hoc duties as required. This position is an individual contributor role reporting to the Director, Workplace Experience. **Responsibility** * Maintain reception desk services including greeting visitors and assisting internal employees while delivering excellent customer service * Build relationships of mutual trust, and handle complaints and concerns in an emotionally intelligent and constructive way * Welcome new hires to the office and familiarize them with the building amenities and where to go when they have questions * Manage office supply inventory * Interface with the senior leadership team professionally and courteously on a daily basis * Support administrative needs for standard office operations as well as recurring or ad hoc projects, including meeting room coordination, reporting on office and conference room utilization, and assisting with local office Intranet site with forward\-thinking ideas and strong written communication skills * Monitor internal feedback and communication channels such as the local Slack channel and team ticketing system (ServiceNow) and escalate findings appropriately * Monitor and audit office cleanliness and functionality and proactively address and communicate any issues * Coordinate with the building property management team and vendors to submit tickets for repairs, maintenance and miscellaneous requests * Serve as subject matter expert for building access and protocols * Apply workplace\-related standards, requirements and practices to ensure fully compliant workplace operations and maintenance * Support vendor management for Workplace Experience Vendors, including janitorial, food and beverage, maintenance vendors, and more * Partner with Human Resources, Employee Resource Groups and other key stakeholders in delivering employee\-centric programs, services, and events, building company culture and strengthening engagement * Assist with improving Docusign’s Environmental Corporate Social Responsibility by finding ways to reduce waste and implement eco\-friendly solutions * Coordinate mail flow in and out of the office and act as FedEx super user * Manage local office access badges and work closely with Security to help maintain a safe environment * Respond to emergencies to provide necessary assistance to employees, customers, and building issues * Deliver an excellent customer experience to employees, visitors and vendors * Champion AI tools as a creative partner to design frictionless office experiences **Job Designation** ------------------- **In Office:** Employee works from the physical workspace 100% of the time. Positions at Docusign are assigned a job designation of either In Office, Hybrid or Remote and are specific to the role/job. Preferred job designations are not guaranteed when changing positions within Docusign. Docusign reserves the right to change a position's job designation depending on business needs and as permitted by local law. **What You Bring** ------------------ **Basic** * 2\+ years of experience in office services, hospitality, reception, and/or facilities * Fluency in English (written and spoken) and experience drafting and reviewing business communications * Experience facilitating meetings and leading team events/meetings **Preferred** * Advanced administrative experience, or experience in facilities planning and maintenance in a business environment * Proactive in seeking out stretch assignments to broaden skill set * Responds positively to continuous change * Team player with a flexible mindset * Excellent verbal and written communication and interpersonal skills * General knowledge of Safety and Security standards and access control * High attention to detail and data accuracy * Ability to manage multiple initiatives simultaneously * Good prioritization and time management skills * Proactive approach to problem\-solving, and ability to make good decisions in a rapidly changing environment * Proficiency with Google Suite, Slack, and space planning software * Ability to work extended hours where necessary **Life At Docusign** -------------------- **Working here** Docusign is committed to building trust and making the world more agreeable for our employees, customers and the communities in which we live and work. You can count on us to listen, be honest, and try our best to do what’s right, every day. At Docusign, everything is equal. We each have a responsibility to ensure every team member has an equal opportunity to succeed, to be heard, to exchange ideas openly, to build lasting relationships, and to do the work of their life. Best of all, you will be able to feel deep pride in the work you do, because your contribution helps us make the world better than we found it. And for that, you’ll be loved by us, our customers, and the world in which we live. **Accommodation** Docusign is committed to providing reasonable accommodations for qualified individuals with disabilities in our job application procedures. If you need such an accommodation, or a religious accommodation, during the application process, please contact us at accommodations@docusign.com. If you experience any issues, concerns, or technical difficulties during the application process please get in touch with our Talent organization at taops@docusign.com for assistance.
